articles.title: FCC to vote on barring sales of devices with parts from blacklisted firms
articles.description: The measure, if approved, will close a "loophole" and "protect Americans from electronic devices that have ​been determined to pose unacceptable risks to the national ⁠security of the ‌United States," the FCC said in a statement.

articles.title: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports
articles.description: Indian auto manufacturers celebrated robust June sales, fueled by strong domestic demand and surging exports. Maruti Suzuki reported over 200,000 units sold, while Mahindra & Mahindra saw a significant 37% growth with over 106,000 vehicles. Hyundai Motor India also posted strong figures, overcoming a temporary production setback due to a supplier issue. These impressive numbers highlight a thriving automotive sector.

articles.title: Gruha Jyothi Yojana: Karnataka launches door-to-door verification
articles.description: The Karnataka Energy Department began a door-to-door verification drive for Gruha Jyothi Yojana beneficiaries. The drive aims to update records for the free electricity scheme. Beneficiaries are asked to keep documents ready for ESCOM staff.

articles.title: Cyber threats now tailored for demographic groups
articles.description: Cybercriminals are increasingly targeting vulnerable groups, weaponizing age and innocence. Senior citizens are falling prey to financial scams, while women face exploitation through intimate data and reputational harm. Most alarming is the sharp surge in child exploitation online, with a dramatic increase in cases involving sexually explicit material. Experts urge caution and verification in online interactions.

articles.description: SpaceX halves the price of Starlink for those living near its data centers in Memphis and Southaven, Mississippi. The company is facing lawsuits over noise and pollution in both regions.

articles.title: New macro indicators in the works to track Viksit Bharat progress
articles.description: India is crafting a robust Viksit Bharat Sankalp indicator framework, featuring around 385 macro indicators, to meticulously track its journey towards becoming a developed nation by 2047. This data-driven mechanism, spearheaded by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, will assess economic, social, and infrastructure progress across key sectors. The comprehensive system aims for implementation by 2031, ensuring inclusive and sustainable development is at the forefront.

articles.title: Samsung confirms the Galaxy Ring 2
articles.description: Samsung launched the Galaxy Ring in 2024, and so you may have been wondering where its successor is. Back in May we heard that the Galaxy Ring 2 would arrive in early 2027 with improved battery life, comfort, and sensor accuracy. Now a new report says Samsung itself has confirmed that the Galaxy Ring 2 is in development, through the voice of Dr. Hon Pak, the company's head of the Digital Health team. The original Samsung Galaxy Ring He strongly hinted at upcoming iPhone pairing support, while confirming that the next Galaxy Ring will have longer battery life and upgraded health...

If you haven't been following the story, keep in mind that the new Galaxy Z Fold8 isn't a direct successor to the Galaxy Z Fold7 from last year. It's actually the previously rumored "Galaxy Fold Wide", which will take the place of the vanilla, while the current Z Fold7's true successor will be the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra. articles.description: Amazon's month-end price cut on Apple's M5 Pro MacBook Pro with an upgrade to 2TB of storage delivers a 15% discount off retail. According to the leak, the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra will retain the Galaxy Z Fold7's triple rear camera setup, but replace the 12MP ultrawide camera with a new 50MP sensor. articles.description: The 2026 Ducati Multistrada V4 Rally is now available for purchase in India, with prices starting at Rs 32.40 lakh for Ducati Red and Rs 32.73 lakh for Jade Green. Both prices are ex-showroom.

articles.title: Delhi EV Policy 2026: Zero road tax, big subsidies, and the end of petrol two-wheelers from THIS date
articles.description: Delhi EV Policy 2026: The policy takes effect on July 1, 2026, and runs until March 31, 2030. The biggest draw is the tax exemption on electric cars, which can save buyers several lakh rupees on a mid-range EV.

articles.title: 'No Steering Wheel, No Pedals': Elon Musk's Tesla Cybercab Video Sparks Huge Safety Debate Online
articles.description: Tesla has started engineering tests of its production Cybercab on public roads in Austin, Texas, with the vehicle operating without a steering wheel or pedals. Elon Musk shared videos of the milestone, sparking widespread discussion online.

articles.title: Draft framework for power data sharing released
articles.description: India is set to standardize electricity sector data sharing with a new national framework. This initiative aims to improve access to vital information on power generation, transmission, and consumption, while safeguarding consumer privacy and critical infrastructure. The proposed system will classify data into public and access-controlled categories, with sensitive details remaining protected. This move promises a more unified and accessible electricity data ecosystem for policymakers and researchers.

articles.title: TSLA Q2 Deliveries May Miss Estimates, But Cantor Says AI, Robotics, Chips Could Drive 'Transformational' 2026
articles.description: The firm expects 397,414 Q2 deliveries, below Tesla’s company-compiled consensus of 408,609.

articles.description: Vikas Nimesh, Assistant Professor at School of Public Policy, IIT Delhi, called the mandate to switch to electric two-wheelers, three-wheelers and school buses in the coming years a "game changer".

articles.title: V-Guard Launches Arizo Series – India’s First Glass Finish Water Heater, Elevating Bathroom Aesthetics
articles.description: Bengaluru: V-Guard, one of India’s most trusted consumer electrical and electronics brands, today announced the launch of the Arizo Series – India’s first glass-finish water heater. Designed for homes who value both aesthetics and performance, the premium storage water heater range seamlessly combines a sophisticated glass-finish façade, intelligent temperature indication, and advanced heating technology, transforming [...] The post V-Guard Launches Arizo Series – India’s First Glass Finish Water Heater, Elevating Bathroom Aesthetics appeared first on FM Live .

articles.title: WhatsApp to let people chat without sharing phone numbers: What that means for a regular user with a phone number
articles.description: WhatsApp is introducing usernames, a significant update allowing users to connect without sharing their phone numbers. This privacy-focused move aligns with platforms like Telegram and Signal, offering greater control over personal information. While a phone number remains necessary for account verification, usernames will be the primary way to initiate chats with new contacts, enhancing security and reducing spam for its over three billion users.

articles.title: Delhi EV Policy 2026 Drops 50% Tax Relief for Strong Hybrid Cars
articles.description: Delhi EV Policy 2026 has rolled back the earlier draft proposal for a 50% road tax and registration cut for strong hybrids under Rs 30 lakh. Here's all you need to know about what the move means for potential hybrid buyers in India.

articles.title: Google brings Gemini's personalised AI image generator to free users
articles.description: Google is expanding one of Gemini's most personalised AI features to more users. Eligible users in the US can now generate customised images for free, with Gemini Nano Banana, using information from connected Google apps to create visuals tailored to individual interests, preferences and even photos stored in Google Photos.

articles.title: Persistent CEO brushes off market rout, says Nagarro growth will reward investors
articles.description: Persistent Systems' shares came under pressure on Monday with investor concerns over the deal's size, execution risks and the uncertainty around integration. The shares ended 11.3% and 11.2% lower at ₹4,292 and ₹4,298 per share on the NSE and BSE, respectively.
